title: Sarga angusta
intro: Sarga angusta, also known as Indian senna or Cassia angustifolia, is a perennial shrub native to India and Africa. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including treating constipation, diarrhea, and skin disorders, as well as lowering blood pressure and cholesterol levels. The bioactive constituents of Sarga angusta include anthraquinones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Sarga angusta is commonly prepared as a decoction, powder, or extract, and is often used in formulations for digestive issues, skin problems, and cardiovascular health.
taxonomy: Sarga angusta is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Poaceae, commonly known as grasses. It belongs to the subfamily Danthonioideae and the genus Sarga, which is native to tropical Africa. The classification of Sarga angusta is further grouped into the kingdom Plantae and the phylum Angiospermophyta. This classification helps to identify the plant's characteristics and its place in the botanical hierarchy. By understanding the classification of Sarga angusta, botanists and researchers can better comprehend its evolution and relationships with other plants.
